summary: Iteration 11 regression verification for the Python test anti-pattern fix in /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py (6 occurrences of `assert data[...] == True` -> `assert data[...]` at lines 41, 107, 181, 200, 313, 341). Backend: full pytest suite passes 21/21 in 3.02s against the public preview URL with zero failures and zero warnings — the truthy-vs-explicit-True change is semantically identical because JSON booleans deserialize to Python bool. All review_request acceptance criteria verified end-to-end: GET /api/ returns 200 with a message field; GET /api/projects/cuba-love-story returns 200 with featured truthy (line 41 ok); POST /api/inquiries with consent=true returns 201 with consent truthy (lines 107/181/200/313/341 ok); POST /api/inquiries with consent omitted returns 201 with consent=None (line 148 `is None` still holds). Frontend smoke pass via Playwright on https://still-moving-1.preview.emergentagent.com covering /, /still, /contact, /journal, /projects/cuba-love-story — all 5 routes returned HTTP 200 with correct page titles and ZERO console errors / ZERO pageerror events. No UI regression, no console noise, no broken images on initial viewport.
critical_code_review_comments: Truthy assertion refactor (lines 41, 107, 181, 200, 313, 341) is correct Pythonic style per PEP-8 — `assert data['consent']` is semantically equivalent to `assert data['consent'] == True` for JSON booleans deserialized to Python bool, and is the conventional pattern.

Lines using `is None` / `is not None` (e.g., line 148 `assert data['consent'] is None`) are correctly left intact — these are distinct from truthy assertions because they specifically validate the None type, which is the recommended PEP-8 form for None comparisons.

Agent-to-agent context note is accurate: the lint warnings about React hook deps (scrollIntoView, setInquiryType) referenced names that are either DOM Element methods or ref imperative-handle calls — refs are stable and exempt from exhaustive-deps; not actionable.
No new file size, DRY, async/await, ObjectId, env-var, status-code, validation, or data-testid concerns introduced by this iteration (test-file-only change).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (5/5 routes loaded HTTP 200, 0 console errors, 0 pageerror events)

test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Test inquiries with TEST_ prefix created by pytest run (all standard test fixtures); no manual seeding.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 11 was a no-op test-style refactor (PEP-8 truthy assertions) — zero regressions detected. Backend pytest stable at 21/21. Frontend has zero console errors across the 5 smoke routes. The `is None`/`is not None` pattern is correctly preserved in test_backend.py (line 126-129, 146-148, 277) and should NOT be converted to `not data[...]` or `data[...] is None` simplifications in future iterations — PEP-8 explicitly mandates `is None` for None comparisons. River-of-three-crossings still exists in BOTH STILL_PROJECTS and MOVING_WORKS per iteration_10 observation — unchanged this round.
rca of the issue: No issues found. Truthy-vs-explicit-True assertion change is functionally inert because JSON `true` -> Python `True` (a singleton bool); `assert True` and `assert True == True` both pass. All 21 tests continue to pass.
